2016-12-25.abstract: Under nearly constant bombardment, the Islamic State is on the run, but the Berlin truck rampage shows it can still inspire terrorist attacks.

2016-12-26.abstract: Some use alliteration (Incredible India), others invoke majesty (Cambodia’s Kingdom of Wonder) and a few (Syria’s Always Beautiful) seem disconnected from reality.

2016-12-26.title: How to Build a Human Tower
2016-12-26.abstract: In a practice unique to Catalonia, people climb on top of each other to create towers called castells, sometimes up to 10 tiers high. Step inside a town square as one goes up.

2016-12-26.title: Russia Mounts Search After Plane Crash and Says Terrorism Is Unlikely
2016-12-26.abstract: A large chunk of the fuselage was located in the Black Sea about a mile offshore, initially by sonar and then by divers, officials said.

2016-12-26.title: How Peace Between Afghanistan and the Taliban Foundered
2016-12-26.abstract: A Norwegian diplomat became a go-between in exploring peace talks. But amid mistrust and military and intelligence maneuvering, it all fell apart.

2016-12-26.title: Portrait of George Michael Fills Out, Showing Pitfalls of Fame and Quiet Generosity
2016-12-26.abstract: While admirers focused on Mr. Michael’s previously unreported donations and philanthropy, a more complicated image of his life in recent years loomed, as well.

2016-12-27.title: A Defiant Israel Vows to Expand Its Settlements
2016-12-27.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu defended his response to the Security Council’s condemnation, saying that Israel does not “turn the other cheek.”

2016-12-27.title: South Korea Scraps Park Geun-hye’s Contentious Textbook Policy
2016-12-27.abstract: The proposal, requiring the use of government-issued history textbooks, became increasingly untenable as a corruption scandal engulfed Ms. Park’s presidency.

2016-12-27.title: Russian Plane’s ‘Black Box’ Found in Black Sea After Crash
2016-12-27.abstract: The flight data recorder may show why the Tupolev 154, which was carrying members of a military choir, crashed en route to Syria, killing 92.

2016-12-27.title: Saudi Royal Family Is Still Spending in an Age of Austerity
2016-12-27.abstract: Low oil prices are forcing questions about whether the huge family can maintain both its lavish lifestyle and its grip on power.

2016-12-27.title: ‘Democratic Spaces’ in La Paz Street Parades
2016-12-27.abstract: Experience the diversity of the Aymara culture on the streets of Bolivia. La Paz is host to more than 1000 street festivals a year, and participants describe them as a safe space to express themselves.

2016-12-28.title: Romania’s President Rejects First Female Candidate for Prime Minister
2016-12-28.abstract: Had Sevil Shhaideh been selected and confirmed, she would have also been the first Muslim to hold the office.

2016-12-28.title: Cristina Fernández de Kirchner Indicted Again on Corruption Charges
2016-12-28.abstract: In her second indictment since she left office last year, Mrs. Kirchner, the former president of Argentina, was indicted Tuesday on charges involving huge public works projects.

2016-12-28.title: John Kerry, in a Final, Pointed Plea, Will Outline a Vision of Mideast Peace
2016-12-28.abstract: Mr. Kerry will use his remarks to confront Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel after bitterness over a United Nations resolution, a State Department official said.

2016-12-28.title: Choir in Plane Crash Projects Russian Pride and Soft Power
2016-12-28.abstract: The Alexandrov Ensemble is known for traditional songs and is not to be confused with the Russian Federation’s police chorus, which sang Pharrell Williams’s “Happy.”

2016-12-28.title: H.I.V. Cases Surpass a Million in Russia, but Little Is Done
2016-12-28.abstract: A combination of stigma and ignorance, a “family values” emphasis and government indifference have pushed H.I.V. to epidemic status.

2016-12-29.title: Vesna Vulovic, Flight Attendant Who Survived Jetliner Blast, Dies at 66
2016-12-29.abstract: Ms. Vulovic, who wasn’t scheduled to be on the flight from Copenhagen to Belgrade in 1972, was the only one of 28 people to survive after plummeting thousands of feet onto a snowy hill.

2016-12-29.title: As China’s Largest Freshwater Lake Shrinks, Solution Faces Criticism
2016-12-29.abstract: A proposed sluice gate faces a chorus of opposition from scientists and environmental groups who say it could have disastrous effects on the lake’s fragile ecosystem.

2016-12-29.title: Kerry’s Blunt Words for Israel Denounced by Lawmakers in Both Parties
2016-12-29.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry’s speech, in which he said “friends need to tell each other the hard truths,” was greeted warmly in Europe but met with a collective shrug in the Arab world.

2016-12-29.title: In John Kerry’s Mideast Speech, a Clash of Policies and Personalities
2016-12-29.abstract: On one side, were Mr. Kerry and President Obama. On the other, President-elect Donald J. Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, and time is on their side.

2016-12-29.title: Genocide’s Legacy: ‘It’s True, the Germans Killed Us’
2016-12-29.abstract: More than 100 years after a genocide of the Herero people in Namibia, Germany is acknowledging its role. Listen to a Herero elder recounting the story he heard from his forefathers.

2016-12-30.title: In ‘Red’ and ‘Blue’ Israel, Separate Lives and Divergent Narratives
2016-12-30.abstract: The reactions to international criticism of Israeli settlements made clear that Israelis are just as polarized as Americans.

2016-12-30.title: American Jews Divided Over Strain in U.S.-Israel Relations
2016-12-30.abstract: While some Jewish groups have applauded the administration’s efforts in regard to Israel, others have seen the steps taken by a departing president as a mistake.

2016-12-30.title: Argentine Court Reopens Investigation of Ex-President in ’94 Bomb Case
2016-12-30.abstract: A court reopened a criminal complaint that accuses former President Cristina Fernández de Kirchner of a cover-up in connection with the 1994 bombing of a Jewish center.

2016-12-30.title: Two Russian Compounds, Caught Up in History’s Echoes
2016-12-30.abstract: East Coast retreats for diplomats were seen by the Obama administration as beachside spy nests, and all Russians were ordered out.

2016-12-30.title: The State of the War in Syria
2016-12-30.abstract: Some of the major developments in Syria surrounding a cease-fire agreement reached Thursday.

2016-12-31.title: Venezuela’s Deadliest Year
2016-12-31.abstract: Amid an economic collapse, a wave of violence is afflicting the country.

2016-12-31.title: ‘The Game Will Go On’ as U.S. Expels Russian Diplomats
2016-12-31.abstract: President Obama’s decision to expel 35 Russian diplomats was provocative, but gamesmanship on both sides involves familiar calculations, former intelligence officers say.

2016-12-31.title: How Russians Pay to Play in Other Countries
2016-12-31.abstract: The Kremlin’s payment of a hefty state fine in the Czech Republic last spring, and the Czech president’s support for Russia, spur cries of influence-buying.

2016-12-31.title: Canada Today: Reporting That Stood Out in 2016
2016-12-31.abstract: Compelling articles examined the automotive tastes of young Chinese immigrants, the deaths of seven indigenous students and the experiences of Syrians settling in Canada.

2016-12-31.title: The Lauded Russian Hacker Whose Company Landed on the U.S. Blacklist
2016-12-31.abstract: The United States’ sanctions list includes the company of a minor celebrity hacker who was once recognized by the American government for her work helping companies fight cybercrime.
